questions.question: Who was South Africa's first Black President?
Whose 2016 presidential campaign slogan was "Make America Great Again"?
The largest consumer market in 2015 was...
Due to the Nagoya Resolution, China agreed to allow Taiwan to compete separately in international sporting events under what name?
Who succeeded Joseph Stalin as General Secretary of the Communist Party of the Soviet Union?
Which nation joined NATO as its 29th member in 2017?
Which country is known for not having a written constitution despite being a constitutional monarchy?
questions.explanation: The United Kingdom is unique in that it does not have a single, written constitution. Instead, its constitutional framework is based on statutes, common law, and conventions. This makes it a notable counterexample to countries with explicit, formal written constitutions.

questions.question: Which constitutional document limited the powers of the English monarchy for the first time in 1215?
questions.explanation: The Magna Carta, signed in 1215 by King John of England, was the first document to impose legal constraints on a monarch's power. It established the principle that the king was subject to the law, a foundational concept for constitutional governance.
